title: Retrospective-phase bridge launches time out en masse at fleet width 10 (exit=81 artifact timeout) — poisons verdicts to FAIL
summary: Live incident 2026-07-12 ~21:00-23:50 (waves of cycles 719-728 and 729-738 at width 10): at least 6 distinct lanes repeatedly logged '[retro] WARN bridge failed (bridge: launch exit=81: source-writing phase "retrospective" has sandbox.allow_network=false; forcing true ...: core: bridge artifact timeout) — emitting FAIL verdict; orchestrator routes via failure-adapter (non-fatal)'. Wave ok-rates collapsed to 3/10 and 2/10 even though build/audit/ship largely succeeded — the retro phase's bridge REPL could not produce its artifact within the timeout under 10 concurrent lanes (likely REPL boot/resource contention; codex family was also quota-benched so claude hosted nearly everything). Two angles to fix the class: (1) retro bridge launch should inherit the failure-adapter's backoff/stagger or a per-family concurrent-REPL semaphore so N simultaneous retro launches don't starve each other; (2) a FAIL verdict emitted solely because the RETRO phase (post-ship, non-source) timed out misclassifies an otherwise-successful cycle — decouple retro-artifact failure from the cycle verdict (retro is already 'non-fatal' per the adapter, but the verdict still lands FAIL). Also note the sandbox.allow_network=false→forcing-true WARN fires on every retro launch — the profile should declare allow_network=true for model-reaching retro instead of relying on the runtime override.
acceptance: RED: TestRetroBridgeTimeout_DoesNotPoisonCycleVerdict (ship succeeded + retro artifact timeout => verdict reflects shipped work, retro failure recorded separately)
RED: TestBridgeLaunch_ConcurrentRetroLaunchesBounded (width-10 fleet => retro REPL launches serialized/bounded, no artifact timeout storm)
RED: retrospective profile declares sandbox.allow_network=true (no runtime forcing WARN)
